critical.mandate: Performance budgets must be defined with measurable thresholds
Load test scenarios must include realistic traffic patterns

step.title: Performance Budget
step.action: Define response time targets: P50, P95, P99 latency
Define throughput targets: requests per second (RPS)
Define error rate thresholds (target less than 0.1% under normal load)
Establish baseline metrics from current production or staging

step.title: Load Test Design
step.action: Load testing/knowledge/performance/k6-patterns.md
Create test scenarios using k6 or equivalent load testing tool
Define virtual user profiles representing real traffic patterns
Design ramp-up strategies: gradual load, spike test, soak test
Define test data requirements and seed data generation

step.title: Frontend Performance
step.action: Load testing/knowledge/performance/lighthouse-ci.md
Define Core Web Vitals targets: LCP under 2.5s, INP under 200ms, CLS under 0.1
Set bundle size budgets per route (JS, CSS, images)
Identify critical rendering path optimizations

step.title: Backend Profiling
step.action: Identify slow queries: N+1 problems, missing indexes, full table scans
Analyze memory allocation patterns and potential leaks
Check connection pool sizing and exhaustion scenarios
Profile CPU-intensive operations and identify optimization targets

step.title: CI Integration
step.action: Add Lighthouse score thresholds to CI pipeline (performance over 90)
Define load test pass/fail criteria for CI gates
Set bundle size limits with automated enforcement
Configure performance regression alerts

step.title: Generate Output
step.action: Generate performance test plan with budgets, load scenarios, and CI gates
